items.title: 'Huge amount' of antibiotics at salmon farm concerning, say Greens, expert

children.content: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

items.title: Electric ferry needs a lift as transport ship stuck in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The world's largest electric ferry is sitting idle in Tasmania after construction was completed because the heavy-lift ship that was booked to take it to South America is stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Memorial held on 30th anniversary of Port Arthur massacre

children.content: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

articles.title: They were the first victims of the Port Arthur massacre. Their family has never forgotten

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

image.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

articles.title: Walter Mikac lost his wife and daughters at Port Arthur. Thirty years on, this is his message

children.content: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

items.title: Who's who in Tasmania's upper house elections this weekend

children.content: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

items.title: This week could see Tasmania's hottest May day in nearly 140 years

children.content: Tasmania is well into autumn, but firefighters remain on alert as late-season bushfires have threatened communities. This Friday marks the first day of May, and with it could come one of the state's hottest May days in nearly 140 years of record-keeping.

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.
